# backend/slack_integration/router.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-13T10:03:12Z

## Purpose
/api/v1/slack/* · Iter 49 · Slack ↔ LLM integration.

Three surfaces:
  · POST /slack/dispatch       · server-side · push notice to a webhook URL
  · POST /slack/command        · receive Slack slash command · run agent · reply
  · POST /slack/ask-agent      · model-backed Q&A · uses Ollama/OpenAI/Anthropic
  · GET  /slack/health         · probe webhook config

Per global §96 + §50 · LLM resolution Ollama → OpenAI → Anthropic → stub.

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import logging
- from `_` import os
- from `_` import time
- from `datetime` import datetime, timezone
- from `_` import httpx
- from `fastapi` import APIRouter
- from `pydantic` import BaseModel

## Inputs
- Env vars: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL

## Process
### Classes
- `SlackDispatch`: (no docstring)
- `SlackCommand`: Mirrors Slack slash command POST body subset.
- `AskAgent`: (no docstring)
### Functions
- `_webhook_url()`: (no docstring)
- `health()`: (no docstring)
- `dispatch(body)`: Send a Slack notification · honest scaffold if webhook unset.
- `slash_command(body)`: Receive Slack slash command · invoke matching agent · reply in-thread.
- `ask_agent(body)`: Direct LLM Q&A via Slack with optional Slack-side notification.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
- Endpoints
- Map slash command → agent
- Invoke via in-process runtime
- Use a minimal skill set
